feat: rendezvous ProviderSummary integration and CI symlink cleanup (Phases 5+9)

Phase 5 — Rendezvous global findability:
- Add `rendezvous_shard_topic_for_agent()` returning "x0x.rendezvous.shard.<u16>"
- Add `Agent::advertise_identity(validity_ms)` — signs and publishes a
  ProviderSummary (with bincode-encoded addresses in extensions field) to the
  rendezvous shard topic for this agent
- Add `Agent::find_agent_rendezvous(agent_id, timeout_secs)` — subscribes to
  the rendezvous shard topic and waits for a matching ProviderSummary
- Extend `Agent::find_agent()` from 2-stage to 3-stage: cache → shard (5s) →
  rendezvous (5s); enables cross-cluster findability
- Add `rendezvous_enabled` and `rendezvous_validity_ms` x0xd config fields
- Initial advertisement at daemon startup + periodic re-advertisement task
- Update saorsa-gossip-* dependencies from 0.5.1 to 0.5.2 (includes
  ProviderSummary.extensions field and sign_raw/verify_raw methods)
- Add 2 new tests: rendezvous shard topic determinism + shard number alignment

Phase 9 — Remove CI symlink workaround:
- Remove checkout + symlink steps for ant-quic and saorsa-gossip from all
  four workflows (ci.yml, release.yml, build.yml, build-bootstrap.yml)
- All dependencies are version deps resolved directly from crates.io; the
  sibling-directory workaround is no longer needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SBOM format incompatibility between sbom-action and scan-action

anchore/sbom-action@v0 was updated to generate CycloneDX 1.6, but
anchore/scan-action@v3 pins Grype v0.74.4 which only supports up to
CycloneDX 1.5 — causing "sbom format not recognized" at scan time.

Switch SBOM format from cyclonedx-json to spdx-json, which both the
current Syft (sbom-action) and Grype v0.74.4 (scan-action) support.

feat: cublasSgemmStridedBatched FFI + safe wrapper for attention GEMMs (Refs #166) (#167)

Adds strided batched GEMM support for multi-head attention:
- cublas_sys.rs: cublasSgemmStridedBatched FFI binding
- cublas.rs: gemm_f32_strided_batched() + row-major convenience wrapper
- batch_count = batch_size * num_heads for attention QK^T and attn·V

Enables cuBLAS tensor cores for attention GEMMs (currently PTX scalar).
Expected 16x speedup for attention GEMM compute (~200ms → ~12ms).

commit.message:
implement all REST endpoints (auth, sessions, events, AI summary)

Layers added:
- exception: ResourceNotFoundException, AIServiceException, GlobalExceptionHandler
- dto: Login, Session, Event, Search, RiskSummary, Error DTOs
- repository: SessionRepository (JpaSpecificationExecutor + JOIN FETCH),
  EventRepository, UserRepository
- mapper: SessionMapper (toResponseDTO with events / toListResponseDTO without),
  EventMapper (session.id → sessionId)
- security: JwtService, JwtAuthenticationFilter, UserDetailsServiceImpl,
  AdminUserSeeder (seeded from APP_ADMIN_USERNAME/PASSWORD env vars)
- service: RiskScoringService (7 rules, capped at 100),
  SessionService, EventService, AIRiskSummaryService (503 on failure)
- controller: AuthController, SessionController, EventController
- config: SecurityConfig (JWT filter wired), CorsConfig, OpenApiConfig (Swagger UI)

commit.message:
ci: fix Azure deployment — use Node 20, npm ci, skip Azure's own build

Azure was likely failing to build with its default Node version.
Now: GitHub Actions installs Node 20, runs npm ci + npm run build locally,
then uploads the pre-built dist with skip_app_build: true.
This guarantees the same build environment as local development.

commit.message:
fix(cluster): defer self-node P2P restart during rolling updates

The update coordinator was killing its own P2P process in batch 1,
then failing convergence checks because localhost:8770 was down.
Now detects self-node via RINGRIFT_NODE_ID/hostname, defers P2P
restart to Phase 4 (after all batches converge successfully).

commit.message:
ODIN-101: Incorporate multi-LLM review findings into streaming concept

Addresses 9 critical findings from ChatGPT and Gemini reviews:
- Producer-decoupling via async dispatch layer (6.5)
- NON-DROPPABLE end-to-end guarantee via EventLog (6.4)
- Replay ordering fix as implementation requirement (7.6)
- Coalescing via latest-value-cache for snapshot events (6.4)
- Stream-gap signaling control event (7.5)
- Wall-clock vs SimClock TTL distinction (6.6)
- Flush-on-pause to prevent stale UI (9.4)
- Frontend memory windowing reference to ODIN-102 (10.5)
- SimTime-based throttling as config option (9.5)

Also adds implementation protocol document.

Full-stack Databricks App with:
- FastAPI backend (Python): campaign management, offer generation, customer intelligence

- Lakebase Postgres persistence for campaigns, offers, and loyalty state
- Unity Catalog integration for live customer data (10,000 shoppers)

- DLT pipeline for real-time intent scoring

commit.message:
fix: graphics capture unwrap bridge response (data.base64)

Bridge wraps response as { success, data: { base64 } } but handlers
checked result.base64 directly, causing false "no image data" error
while still returning the image. Now checks data.base64 first.

commit.message:
fix: handle query task errors in SSE stream gracefully

When summarize_question() or retrieve_entries() raised an exception, the
SSE generator crashed after the event queue drained, sending no final
events. The frontend saw the stream close with nodes animated but no
response panel or error message.

Now catch exceptions from the query task and yield an error SSE event +
stream_end so the frontend can show feedback. Add error event handler
in the frontend status line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix PR review issues: migration safety, resolver warnings, test coverage

- Migration: add pre-flight check for orphaned/NULL caliber refs before
  backfill; use ABS() tolerance in downgrade and verify before NOT NULL
- Resolver: warn when diameter filter is skipped or caliber lookup fails
- Pipeline store: guard against None bullet_diameter_inches before create
- describe_db.py: replace stale caliber_id JOINs with diameter grouping
- Update resolver docstring to reflect diameter-based bullet matching
- Add 14 resolver tests covering resolve("bullet"), diameter tolerance
  boundaries, cross-caliber prevention, cartridge-to-bullet via diameter
- Use .between() instead of exact float equality in seed data test

  Section navigation clears only the current lead's history.

  total_sections, chunk_idx, total_chunks, flow_step); the OpenAI
  system prompt is enriched with a concise progress line when provided.
  Deterministic fallback is unchanged.

  steps (was missing from reflection).

  regressions beyond the pre-existing date-sensitivity failure).

commit.message:
Fix sticky stack: remove overflow:hidden and will-change that broke position:sticky

Both properties prevented sections from sticking to the viewport correctly.
overflow:hidden creates a new scroll container; will-change:transform breaks
sticky on Safari and Chrome compositor layers.

commit.message:
style: torn paper strip with tape for day descriptions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve user-provided task IDs in batch createTasks dependencies (#479)

When AI models call createTasks with user-provided IDs (e.g., "auth",
"list-projects") and reference them in dependencies, the batch would
crash because createTask ignores custom IDs and auto-generates them.

Now createTasks maps user-provided IDs to auto-generated IDs before
creating tasks, and the entire batch is atomic — if any task fails
validation, no tasks are created.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
